what: CPU thread-parallelism correctness gate for GGML_OP_ATTN_SCORE_QJL + GGML_OP_FUSED_ATTN_QJL_TBQ in the built dflash fork lib
host: Intel Arrow Lake-HX, 24 cores, AVX2 + AVX-VNNI (no AVX-512), 30 GB RAM

build.result: all CMake targets built + linked OK (libggml-cpu.so, libggml-base.so, llama-cli, llama-server, llama-speculative-simple); process exited non-zero ONLY on the documented probeKernels runtime-dispatch evidence gate (turbo3_tcq / qjl_full / polarquant), which is the same expected blocker as the Vulkan/CPU paths — the libs were still emitted
patchersApplied: cpu-polar-kernels.mjs (PolarQuant _preht TU wiring — NEW this commit)
cpu-thread-parallelism.mjs (QJL attn ops ith/nth split — NEW this commit)

shippedLib.note: ggml_vec_dot_q4_polar_preht_f32_neon correctly absent on x86 (compiled out by __ARM_NEON guard); _avx2 body present => POLARQUANT_HAVE_AVX2 wired

mtVsStGate.method: build a ggml graph using each op, run ggml_backend_graph_compute with n_threads=1 then n_threads=24 against the BUILT libggml-cpu.so, compare output bit-for-bit
mtVsStGate.verdict: PASS — bit-identical (the thread split is over disjoint output rows/heads, no reduction => no FP reassociation => exact equality)

referenceTest: make -C packages/inference/verify reference-test => clean (all kernels finite, fused-attn + tbq V-cache parity OK)
kernelContract: make -C packages/inference/verify kernel-contract => OK kernels=6 targets=23 manifestNames=6
cpuBench: make -C packages/inference/verify cpu-bench && ./cpu_bench --runs 3 --warmup 1 => bench_results/cpu_d3_thread_parallelism_2026-05-11.json (single-thread C reference harness; not the built lib)
